description: A piece of land surrounded by water. 'Mola' names the land that stands alone—complete in itself, defined by the waters that embrace it, a world unto itself.
sound_symbolism: The word holds land in water. 'Mo' begins in the midst, surrounded by sea. Then 'la' lands firmly, the earth standing despite the water.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a noun: 'lo mola' (the island). Takes the elemental classifier 'themo' when counted. Associated with isolation, completeness, and self-contained ecosystems.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Islands teach self-reliance and completeness—the Buddha spoke of being 'an island unto oneself,' finding refuge within.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Island peoples developed unique cultures through isolation—completeness within limits, adaptation to bounded resources.
